title: Evaluate Rules
description: Schema for hand-authored anti-hallucination rules consumed by /evaluate. Drift between this schema and evaluate-rules.json must fail CI.

version.description: Schema version of this rules file. Bump on breaking shape changes.

source.description: Path to the prose ruleset this file derives from.

id.description: Stable kebab-case identifier. Used by mark-evaluate-task.js to reference rules.

summary.description: One-paragraph plain-language statement of the rule.

source_section.description: Section heading in the prose source file this rule derives from.

trigger.description: Plain-language condition under which the rule fires during an evaluation run.

method.description: Mechanical category of verification. tool_call = single artifact lookup; comparison = two-source diff; enumeration = list-every-item.

allowed_tools.description: Read-only tools the helper script will accept as evidence sources. Mutating tools are deliberately excluded.

must_produce.description: Concrete artifact that must exist in the run transcript for the rule to be marked satisfied. This is the contract mark-evaluate-task.js checks.

exit_condition.description: Mechanical predicate that, when true, means the rule has been satisfied for the current evaluation target.

fallback.description: What the evaluator must do when verification cannot be produced. Must be a non-mutating action (label, omit, halt).

requires_claim.description: If true, mark-evaluate-task.js requires an additional --claim <text> argument beyond --evidence. The claim is the specific assertion being made; the helper uses it for content binding and run-log entries.

evidence_must_contain_claim.description: If true, the --evidence file's contents must contain the --claim text verbatim (case-sensitive substring match). Forces the evidence file to actually support the specific claim, not just exist.

evidence_patterns.description: Per-rule evidence contract (#2288). Binds the --evidence path to the specific claim via the --claim flag. Closes the 'file exists but doesn't support the claim' hole exposed by the 2026-04-06 /evaluate meta-run.

fallback_action.description: Mechanically enforced fallback when the rule cannot be satisfied (#2288). label_unverifiable = move the claim to the ## Unverifiable section. halt_and_report = evaluation must halt; no final verdict allowed (enforced by --finalize). omit_claim = remove the claim from output entirely. conflict_unresolved = report both conflicting values without picking one.
